message:
fix(rchd): add timeout for optional timing body read in release-worker

The release-worker API now optionally accepts a JSON body with timing
breakdown data. However, using read_line() without a timeout would block
indefinitely if the client didn't send a body line.

Changes:
1. api.rs: Wrap read_line() in a 50ms timeout. This is long enough for
   any immediately-available body data but short enough to not delay
   responses when no body is sent.

2. e2e_daemon.rs: Update test to include trailing newline in release
   request, ensuring proper protocol compliance with the body-reading
   change.

This prevents the daemon from hanging on release-worker requests that
don't include timing data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
